rationale: This is the case closest to active learning among the six (a sequential hypothesize-test-update-model cycle, C05-P12/C05-P27), which is exactly why the BED lens must decompose it rather than label it, and why keeping the discrimination OBJECTIVE separate from the candidate rules matters here. control_dependence: fixed_or_predefined -- C05-P13/P14/P15/P16 describe a deterministic four-step bioinformatics hypothesis-generation method (identify orphan E.C. numbers; collect cross-organism query sequences from all KEGG organisms; run PSI-BLAST/FASTA similarity search; form ORF-to-E.C. hypotheses) applied uniformly, not gated by any evaluation of the resulting hypotheses; the source CONFIRMS this outcome-independence (the procedure is stated as fixed steps), so it is fixed_or_predefined, not not_reported. score -- C05-P15 (PSI-BLAST/FASTA 'identify the most similar sequences/ORFs', a similarity-score-ranked selection of the query sequences that feed hypothesis formation). observation_result -- C05-P12 ('Scientific knowledge gained from this process is used to update the model of yeast metabolism'), C05-P27 ('manually updating our system model where conflicts were noticed, before allowing automated generation of hypotheses' -- the next hypothesis-generation round is gated on the updated model). No failure axis: no repair/error-driven selection is reported (matching the absence of any repair objective below). selection_signal names these drivers. selection_objective: uncertainty_reduction -- C05-P17 (microplate layouts use 'Latin-square design to improve the detection of quantitatively small differences above the background noise', an explicit measurement-noise/statistical-power aim); C05-P19/P28 (experiment parameters are 'analysed using machine learning ... to obtain statistically significant results'). hypothesis_model_discrimination -- C05-P19/P28 (results 'can be used to either confirm or refute hypotheses'), C05-P04 (this hypothesis-generation process 'is abductive'), C05-P12/P27 (the model of yeast metabolism is updated from what the experiments discriminate). Note the analytic point that the v3 schema keeps clean: C05 has a genuine discrimination OBJECTIVE, yet its candidate rules are exhaustive/all_selected, not an expected-discrimination down-selection -- objective and rule are separate axes and do not collapse. generation_scope: fixed_space -- the four-step generation method is a fixed deterministic procedure (C05-P13/P14/P15/P16); the KEGG search ranges over all organisms but is a fixed similarity method, and no novelty-directed search-space-expansion or candidate-diversification mechanism is reported (contrast C03/C04/C06). candidate_selection_rule: exhaustive -- C05-P22 ('Adam ... tested all these hypotheses on its robotic system'; all 20 conceived hypotheses were tested), direct evidence against any expected-information-gain-driven down-selection of which hypotheses to test. The similarity-score ranking (C05-P15) selects query SEQUENCES for hypothesis formation (the generation-stage 'score' signal above) but does not down-select which HYPOTHESES are executed. candidate_execution_rule: all_selected -- every conceived hypothesis is executed on the robotic system (C05-P22), the direct execution-rule counterpart of the exhaustive selection. No objective label appears in either rule. formal_epistemic_utility_evidence: not_reported -- despite the abductive framing (C05-P04) and the uncertainty/discrimination aims, the bounded source states no prior/posterior, likelihood, EIG, or expected-epistemic-utility term in any selection rule; this divergence between an active-learning-shaped aim and a formal BED selection rule is a corpus-level limitation stated once, not a per-case verdict.
